g.title: DynamicWorkflowArch
g.text: Multi-Agent Harness — Dynamic Workflow Runtime (skill + CLI)
Any agent (Codex / Claude Code / other) authors a JSON workflow spec and runs it through a Rust runtime that orchestrates other agents.
Green = exists today · Blue = to build · dashed grey = optional future shim.

g.title: cluster_runtime
g.text: 3 · harness-workflow (NEW Rust lib crate — extracted from harness-cli, provider-agnostic)

g.title: cluster_seam
g.text: 4 · Real driver + delivery seam (EXISTS — harness-cli injects it into the runtime)

g.title: cluster_workers
g.text: 5 · Worker agents (the orchestrated subagents — headless, fully captured)

g.title: skill
g.text: teaches the agent to WRITE a valid spec + invoke the CLI + read back the run

g.title: cli
g.text: the contract / entry point · harness-cli bin

g.title: other
g.text: anything that can run a shell cmd

g.title: optmcp
g.text: only if an agent has NO shell, or for external-project distribution;
thin wrapper over the same CLI — NOT built now

g.title: interp
g.text: walks the IR; applies phase / barrier / stream semantics

g.title: sched
g.text: min(16, cores-2) permits + 1000 lifetime cap · workflow.rs:98

g.title: prims
g.text: parallel() EXISTS · pipeline() to implement · workflow.rs:163/229

g.title: stepfn
g.text: runtime stays provider-agnostic; the bin injects the real driver

g.title: driver
g.text: one agent() node → one harness Message

g.title: claim
g.text: atomic claim/lease under the store lock

g.title: deliver
g.text: picks Codex or Claude adapter per node's member

g.title: drill
g.text: click node → TurnDrillIn shows that worker's streamed
tool calls (component EXISTS, wire it up)
